items.title: Goodbye colonoscopy? New stool test detects 90% of colorectal cancers
items.summary: A breakthrough in microbiome research could change how colorectal cancer is detected—no colonoscopy required. Scientists used AI to map gut bacteria at an unprecedented level of detail, revealing subtle microbial patterns linked to cancer. By analyzing simple stool samples, their method identified 9

items.title: Scientists finally uncover why promising cancer drugs keep failing
items.summary: Cancer drugs known as BET inhibitors once looked like a breakthrough, but in real patients they’ve often fallen short. New research reveals a key reason why: two closely related proteins, BRD2 and BRD4, don’t actually do the same job. Instead, BRD2 acts like a “stage manager,” preparing genes for ac

items.title: Dragonflies can see a color humans can’t and it could change medicine
items.summary: Dragonflies may see the world in a way that pushes beyond human limits—and surprisingly, they do it using the same molecular trick we evolved ourselves. Scientists discovered that these insects can detect extremely deep red light, even edging into near-infrared, thanks to a specialized visual protei

items.title: Scientists discover spice synergy that boosts anti-inflammation 100x
items.summary: Chronic inflammation often works quietly in the background but can fuel serious diseases like diabetes, heart disease, and cancer. New research reveals that everyday plant compounds—like menthol from mint, cineole from eucalyptus, and capsaicin from chili peppers—can team up inside immune cells to d

items.title: Gravitational waves may be hidden in the light atoms emit
items.summary: Scientists have proposed a surprising new way to detect gravitational waves—by observing how they change the light emitted by atoms.
